summary: Mellow 1973 show with a nice Eyes‑Stella transition and solid Bird Song, but plagued by audience splices, dropouts and horn arrangements that hurt the listening experience.
review: The performance is an average‑to‑good Dead set that shines in moments like the seamless Eyes of the World → Stella Blue segue and a pleasant Bird Song, yet the added horns on Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow are divisive. The recording is an audience tape with noticeable splices and audio dropouts (Bertha, El Paso, Ramblin' Rose, and a missing Don't Ease Me In), which drags the overall enjoyment down.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is around average (≈3/5) while recording issues lower it further; combined weighted score yields ~2.8 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple audience splices and dropouts (Bertha, El Paso, Ramblin' Rose) plus a complete cut of "Don't Ease Me In"; overall muddiness and inconsistent levels.

standout_songs: Eyes of the World > Stella Blue
show_quality.setlist_flow: Laid‑back, reminiscent of 1974 vibe; starts rough due to audio glitches but smooths after "Ramblin' Rose", with a strong mid‑show transition and mellow finish.
